module_mood_contract: M0 prework — mechanical readiness; not on the mood arc. M1 opens with joyful creation; prework primes the student to arrive ready (session in chosen repo, two curated security skills installed, one trivial bug picked, content folder on disk). Step 4 is the sole teaching beat — operator-first delegation + one-at-a-time push-back rehearsal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 applies to teaching beats only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block + trace name the contract: prework is M0 mechanical readiness; Step 4 is the sole teaching beat (mood_score 8/10).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood, not punitive.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 4 closes with peer-warm italic 'Small steer, small trick. A minute here, another there. You find them.' — never shame-shaped.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Arrival statement leads with positive disciplines (session started, skills installed, bug picked) — never with failure-word framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework doesn't lecture on M1 framing ('The framing lands live in Module 1; no pre-read.'). Skills installed by name (access-control-analysis, stride) but M3 owns the security teaching — prework only ships the install.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework carries no Key Concepts section by design — M0 setup, not a module on the arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No Key Concepts section in prework.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No What You'll Learn section in prework; the arrival statement at line 7 is operational, not capability-register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No lecture in prework; strategy doc explicitly defers M1 framing to live delivery.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No lecture in prework.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three named prompts (ae101-prework-download-tarball, ae101-prework-extract-and-install, ae101-prework-one-at-a-time) carry the load-bearing moves. Body callouts ('push back if it dumps all four criteria at once', 'Trivial means a few lines here and there') are recovery-instinct callouts per the rule 16 carve-out + pedagogy §56 (engineer-audience body-callout default).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversations / pause cues / share-with-room.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No banned shapes present. The fallback-repo italic 'Ask your trainer' is operator-routing, not cohort-management.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Task-selection floor AND ceiling explicit.
rules_evaluated.evidence: What-to-bring names trivial floor explicitly (line 13) AND M4 ceiling explicitly (line 16: 'epic-shaped or refactor-shaped'). Step 4 reinforces with 'Trivial means a few lines here and there, not a multi-file refactor.' Floor + ceiling both present in body, not just maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Symmetric visible-mechanics over runtime fork.
rules_evaluated.evidence: AE101 is Code-only; the 3.a/3.b fork is transport-shape (manual vs agentic), not runtime-shape. Maintainer block names this correctly as transport primitive.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session widget shape + placement.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 2 (line 38) carries '**Session** *(new, "prework")*' at canonical exercise-top default placement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body lead-ins around the three prompts name artefacts and activities (extract, install, screen, confirm) — not what the prompt will reveal or surface.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 4 uses body callouts for recoverable failure modes (push-back, trivial-means). Forcing-function escalation reserved for the one-at-a-time prompt where silent failure would propagate downstream.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: <CONTENT_URL> in body (line 46) is a build-time-substituted placeholder per maintainer-block carve-out — never appears inside a fenced prompt block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 3 lead-in (line 48) precedes the download prompt; Step 4 lead-in (line 53) precedes the extract-and-install prompt; Step 4 conditional lead-in (line 62) precedes the one-at-a-time prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Question-dump default — two legal shapes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body callout shape (rule 15b) applied: 'ask Claude to use the AskUserQuestion tool, or to give you the candidate-bug screen one bug at a time. Your call.' Hands the student the choice; right register for engineer audience.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body lead-in mirrors prompt's opening phrasing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body lead-ins use the same verb-noun phrasing the prompts open with ('Ask Claude to fetch...' / 'Ask Claude to extract...'). Phrasal congruence holds.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands: 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[6].mood_score=8 (Step 4 sole teaching beat); close.mood_score=8. Phases 1-5 and 7 mood-exempt (setup / transition beats per check_strategy_tie_in §1 carve-out).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — preserves next module's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework is pre-arc M0; no mood to stew. Hands off to M1 joyful-creation open. The Connections question is queued, not pre-answered.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 4's operator-first delegation + one-at-a-time push-back lands via three named prompts; fast-operator persona walks the chain without confusion; novice persona has the fallback prompt for the question-dump case. Install blockers + Slack helper handle the IT-locked path (maintainer block).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors strategy doc framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc § Delivery architecture: 'Prework is agentic end-to-end. No "create this file, paste this content, commit these lines."' File honors this — Steps 0, 1, 2, 4 are agentic; Step 3 ships the manual transport fallback as an explicit transport-primitive carve-out (named in maintainer block, line 89).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: '## Bring to Module 1' (line 68) names the Connections question matching strategy-doc M1 open hook ('Ex1 is a read-and-introspect move'). Arrival kit (session + skills + bug + trick) feeds M1 directly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing function lives in prompt fence, not body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: One-at-a-time push-back encoded in ae101-prework-one-at-a-time prompt block; extract-and-install sequence encoded in ae101-prework-extract-and-install. Body prose names the moves semantically but the prompts enact them (rule 16 carve-out for optional recovery body callout applies to the trivial-means and push-back nudges).
